Week 5 — Answers & Solutions

Grade 4 · Math Challenge
Problem 1
On what floor does Maia live?
Answer: Floor 4
Jaylon is on floor 2. Pablo is above Jaylon → floor 3. Maia is above Pablo → floor 4. Rafael is above Maia → floor 5.
Problem 2
Area of the shaded square?
Answer: 16
Start from the smallest squares (area 1, side 1) and work up to the larger ones. The shaded square turns out to be 4 × 4 = 16.
Problem 3
How many cookies did Amelia bake?
Answer: 16
Work backwards. Amelia has 2 cookies left, so before she gave her sister half she had 2 × 2 = 4. Before she ate 4 she had 4 + 4 = 8. Before she gave her friend half she had 8 + 8 = 16.
Problem 4
How many draws guarantee a matching pair?
Answer: 4
Suppose she is unlucky and her first three socks are all different colours. There are only 3 colours, so the 4th sock must repeat one — a guaranteed pair. So 4 draws are always enough.
Problem 5
Which ball is the heaviest?
Answer: 4
Read the scales one at a time:
• The 2nd scale leans right → ball 3 is heavier than ball 2.
• The 3rd scale is balanced → ball 3 weighs the same as ball 1.
• The 1st scale leans left → ball 4 is heavier than ball 1 — and since ball 1 = ball 3, ball 4 is heavier than ball 3 too.
So, from heaviest to lightest:The heaviest is ball 4.
Problem 6
How many children are in the line?
Answer: 17
Make a quick sketch. Aarav is 6th from the front. Ivaan is 8th from the end, with 3 children between them, so Ivaan is 10th from the front. Counting everyone gives 17 children.
